place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
building's exterior may look unassuming or even a lil spooky but the inside is really cozy and homely! lovely space, limited tables but plenty of seats

if you really want a table, they have a table booking system you can use to ensure you have one when you get there!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I alighted at this bus interchange (the newer one as opposed to the older "Temporary" one) getting off Bus number 961 from Hillview where I live. I arrived here to visit Causeway Point that is just "above and next to" this bus interchange. Unlike the older "Temporary" bus interchange, this one is new and nicer in terms of comfort as it is air-conditioned and access to Causeway Point is by a convenient set of escalators.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The bus driver seemed half asleep; he was driving incredibly slowly. A bus is meant to get people to their destination quickly, not to make them late! This is terrible! Does he not know how to drive a bus, or is he just sleepy?

It has been years since my last visit, and it now appears to have expanded, with an entire upper floor dedicated to the Children’s Section.
Clean, well-maintained, and serene, it’s a place where one can truly enjoy a sense of calm and peacefulness.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Choa Chu Kang Public Library at Lot One is a very modern, clean, and well-designed library that is conveniently located right next to the MRT station and inside the mall. The interior feels bright and welcoming, with spacious layouts and comfortable areas for reading and studying

I especially liked the contemporary design touches and the creative displays throughout the library, which make the space feel more vibrant and inviting. Despite being in a busy suburban mall, the library still maintains a calm and relaxing atmosphere

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Pleased with my visit here and how clean, modern and spacious the library is. Plenty of seating areas and even a level 5 kids area to entertain the kids. Great selection of books and many assortment of seats to accommodate many people. I also liked that borrowing a book via the NLB app was such a breeze!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Choa Chu Kang Public Library is a well-designed and cozy space for readers of all ages. The library has a good collection of books, magazines, and digital resources, along with plenty of comfortable seating for studying or reading quietly. The layout is modern, spacious, and family-friendly, with dedicated sections for children and teens. It’s also very convenient since it is located inside Lot 1 Shopper’s Mall, making it easy to drop by after shopping or dining. The staff are polite and helpful, and the environment is peaceful — a perfect spot for learning, reading, or simply relaxing.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I think that the Choa Chu Kang Library is very spacious, especially when I walked in. Once you walk around the fiction shelves, you’ll notice that there’s this seating area of 6 chairs around this round table. That’s a good place to work on your computers, study, and read.

Once you see that, just walk towards the window and look left and right, you’ll notice there’s this long table connected to the wall, and multiple people can sit there. That’s a really nice place for reading, studying, and working on your computers. The view is also nice when you look out the window, depending on where you’re sitting though.

They have this specific Malay section of books, and there’s 3 small sections that are kind of curved in, and about 4-5 people could fit in there. Once you walk a bit farther, there’s this section with teen books, and you’ll have to go down a few steps, but it has a really nice section.

Once you’ve passed the teen section, you’ll see stairs going up. If you go up those stairs, you’ll see the children’s section. First you’ll see the family and parenting section; then to the right, you’ll see the children's books.

Overall, I think the Choa Chu Kang Library is nice and spacious, and has a good selection of different books. Nice area for sitting. A good place to read, study, and work on your computers.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Kranji MRT station is a convenient and well-maintained transit point that serves commuters traveling between northern Singapore and the rest of Singapore. The station is especially valued for its practical layout, clean surroundings, and smooth passenger flow, making daily travel comfortable and efficient for both residents and visitors.
One of the station’s strongest advantages is its accessibility. It is strategically located near important facilities, checkpoints, and industrial areas, making it a key stop for workers, students, and travelers. Signage inside the station is clear and easy to follow, which helps first-time users navigate without confusion. Ticketing machines, gantries, and platforms are arranged logically, so even during peak hours movement remains relatively smooth and organized.
Passengers often appreciate the station’s cleanliness and orderly environment. The platforms are spacious and well-ventilated, and safety markings are clearly visible. Staff presence and surveillance systems contribute to a secure atmosphere, giving commuters peace of mind while waiting for trains. The surrounding area is also straightforward to navigate, with bus connections and taxi points located nearby for easy transfers.
Another positive aspect is the calm and less crowded feel compared to major interchange stations. Many commuters find this refreshing, especially during morning and evening travel, as it allows for a more relaxed commuting experience. The station’s simple design and functional layout reflect efficiency rather than complexity, which many users prefer.
Overall, Kranji MRT station is a reliable, commuter-friendly transit stop that offers convenience, cleanliness, and accessibility. While it may not be the busiest or most glamorous station, its practicality and smooth operations make it an important and appreciated part of Singapore’s public transport network.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops

Assistant Station Manager Syahirah assisted me with finding my direction.  She showed an exemplary customer service attitude.  SMRT is lucky to have her.  Great job Syahirah..!!!  👍🏾👍🏾👍🏾

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Internet, chatgbt, google, Ai and the pervasive mobile devices may have changed the way we seek answers. But a library is where serious learnings and discoveries are found.

They are depository of knowledge, curated and preserved. The Central Library is that - and more.

There are librarians dedicated to the preservation of knowledge and their proliferation. Here, they are not stuffy and grumpy matrons. They are professionals and helpful in a way we boomers never knew.

Tell them a hypothesis and they can help you turn an idea into blueprint. Need to fill a gap in citations, they can find you a landfill.

History is our heritage. Lessons learned to inform us. Latest technology is flashy but libraries tell us how they came about.

The Central Library is more than a meeting place for varsity students. Here is where a thousand nuggets of wisdom and knowledge exists - waiting for those with inquiring minds to be transformed into “professors.”

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
lots of study spots! most of the library is for nus students/staff only as there are gantries at the entrance which mainly open with student/staff passes

most study spots have charging spots, each level has a bathroom and water dispensers. there's also food options at level 1 and a bookstore with essentials

only complaint is that the wifi can be quite slow/spotty at times. also level 6 study area can get quite warm sometimes when its busy, like the ac isnt strong enough :")))

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I spend a lot of time in this library when I did my undergrad and post grad studies. It has many books which were not available in other libraries. I did join as a corporate member for a few years too.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Beautiful and modern library with a very rich collection of books and ample seating space.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I entered as an alumnus. I needed an NUS alumnus lifelong email address and also had to download and install the UNivUS app, which generates the QR code for me to enter the library. There was mobile data signal at the registration counter therefore I needed to log into NUG Guest wifi. But the NUS Guest wifi needed an OTP which required mobile signal! Therefore I needed to go back and forth between the Forum (mobile signal to get OTP) and library registration desk (for wifi). Altogether, it took me perhaps half an hour to sort it a out and finally enter the library. Future trips should be straightforward I hope!!! General stacks on 5th floor. Photocopier is on 5th floor but in the Chinese SEA Collection. Use your EZ-link card. $0.03 per A4 - great value!  :-)  The facilities are lovely -wow... compared to 1990s! Love the place. The Coop had lots of cute merch too. Ah... to be a Gen Z or Gen Alpha... 😄😄😄

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Buona Vista MRT Station is one of the most convenient and well-designed stations in Singapore. It serves as an interchange for the East-West Line (EWL) and the Circle Line (CCL), making it an essential hub for commuters traveling across different parts of the island.

The station is clean, spacious, and efficiently managed. Clear signage and well-maintained facilities make transferring between lines smooth and hassle-free.

Its location is a big plus—Buona Vista is close to key areas like one-north, where many tech and research companies are based, and the Star Vista shopping mall, which offers great dining and retail options.

During peak hours, it can get a little crowded, but the station handles the traffic well. Overall, a reliable and strategically located MRT station that’s great for daily commuting and exploring the area!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Buona Vista MRT Station is a very clean and convenient interchange between the East–West Line and Circle Line. Transfers are easy, and the station has clear signs, lifts, and good accessibility. It’s also right next to The Star Vista and one-north, so there are plenty of food and shopping options nearby. A reliable and well-maintained station overall.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Great station - close to the Star Vista Mall where you can find food, drinks, Cold Storage. It serves Green & Yellow lines although it can be confusing to navigate from one to the other.
There are two restaurants, two tea places and convenience store at the station.
There are two bike stations (front and back).

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: There are no sheltered bike parking lots. Is all uncovered and exposed to the elements. If locking the bike at the walkway poles (outside of the mrt station mind you), the staff are so free that they have time to complain. It’s not even part of the mrt station. Perhaps y’all should rethink what are important matters in your job and what is just being a hindrance to people trying to live a healthy and eco friendly life.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
A crowded station with 2 MRT lines - the circle line and east west line. My husband and I observed the circle line area and feel that there are insufficient seating area at the circle line platform.

The station can be enhanced by putting up more seats at the circle line platforms to better serve the needs of commuters waiting for the trains.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Hidden away in the Rifle Range greenery, Temasek Club honestly feels like one of those underrated chill spots in Singapore. The whole place has a peaceful resort vibe with lots of nature surrounding it, making it great for family gatherings, weddings, casual dinners or even just escaping the city for awhile.

The atmosphere is probably the biggest selling point here. Many visitors mention the relaxing ambience, lush views and spacious environment that makes it feel less hectic than most clubs in Singapore. There is also a decent Cantonese restaurant within with very nice menu options.

One thing to note is accessibility. If you are driving, parking is manageable and convenient. But if you rely on public transport, getting there can feel slightly out of the way since the club sits deeper into Rifle Range Road.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: On 6 March 2026, I had the privilege of visiting Temasek Club during a Taiwan–Singapore exchange meeting. The venue is beautiful and well maintained, creating a welcoming and refined atmosphere. The service was professional and attentive, and the food was excellent. It was a very pleasant and memorable experience.

review_text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Jurong West Swimming Complex — Hidden Gem Alert

Best Sunday experience, hands down. Not crazy crowded, which is a rare win in Singapore. The lap pool is bliss—can actually swim freely without dodging elbows. Bonus: it’s shaded from the sun, so no need to fry while doing laps.

Even under CAT1 lightning alerts, you can still swim (huge plus). The kids’ pool is a comfortable 0.9m depth, perfect for little ones. And yes—warm showers in the children’s facilities. Game changer.

There’s a decent water park setup for kids and a surprisingly long water slide that’s way more fun than expected. Honestly, this place is the most underrated swimming complex in Singapore. Total hidden gem. Don’t sleep on it.

review_text:
Civil Service Club Bukit Batok swimming pool.

A variety of fun and relaxing water activities, but access is limited to members and their guests.

The pool area includes a large children's pool, and a wave pool that starts at 10am and runs for 5 minutes every hour—check the website for the schedule. For $3 in cash, you can rent a float, which is required for the towel slide.

The lazy river provides a nice spot to lounge, while kids can enjoy the water playground and a simple water slide on the second level. Swimmers also have access to a lap pool located upstairs.

Hot water showers, hair dryer, and locker available.

review_text:
🌿Choa Chu Kang Park is situated opposite Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange along Choa Chu Kang Drive.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️It's a community garden within the Choa Chu Kang Estate where residents gather for morning workout, walk, jog and do some gardening.
🌿This green oasis offers a relief to the hectic city pace.
🛝🐕‍🦺🍆Like many community gardens, CCK Park too have its fair share of Playground, Fitness Corners, Dog Run, Allotment Garden, Open Lawn and Amphitheatre.
🛼🛹🌶️Besides these regular features, CCK Park have a Skate Plaza and a Community Garden.
🍂Also, somewhat hidden from view, is an Old Tomb Trail.
🚍How to get here:
Exit from Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange to Choa Chu Kang Drive.
Cross over the road.
Park is next to Shell Station.

review_text:
Can an urban park be a space for relaxation, education and creativity? Can it become a unique attraction of the region? Or maybe you are looking for a business idea and want to create an outdoor eductation center? Bet on an interactive park of experiences!

Families are increasingly looking for unusual forms of recreation. When traveling on a trip, they want to not only visit the beautiful old town or museum, but also to provide their children interesting experiences, combined with education. Experience Park is a place where children will not be bored!

The open-air museum will provide great entertainment and learning through play. You can touch each exhibit, run it and learn about the laws of nature through the individual experimentation.

Until now, city parks were equipped with benches, fountains, and nature monuments. Recently, you can also work out in the outdoor gym. It is worth to equip this space with educational devices and in addition to muscle training, practice the mind as well! We suggest creating an educational path, which may consist of several thematic zones, located in different parts of the park.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
AMK Hub is one of the most convenient and practical malls in Ang Mo Kio. The location is excellent, as it is directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT station and the bus interchange, making it very easy to reach by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, dining options, supermarkets, daily essentials, and services. It is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents living nearby. Whether you are looking for groceries, a quick meal, shopping, banking services, or just a place to walk around, AMK Hub has almost everything you need in one location.

I like that the mall is very accessible and easy to navigate. There are many food choices, from fast food and takeaway options to cafés and restaurants for proper meals. It is also a convenient place to run errands, meet friends, or spend time with family.

Although the mall can get quite crowded during weekends and meal times, the overall environment is still lively, clean, and well maintained. The air-conditioning is comfortable, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it easy to move around.

Overall, AMK Hub is a reliable and convenient neighbourhood mall with a good variety of food, shopping, and essential services. It is definitely one of the best places to visit in Ang Mo Kio for everyday needs, meals, and casual shopping.

There’s a wide range of food options here—from food courts with local and international dishes to plenty of fast-food and restaurant chains. It’s not as exciting as the bigger malls, but it’s great for everyday needs and casual dining.

However, the distance taken to walk there is very long as it is very far away from woodlands mrt so that's why it's not a 5 star.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Nov 2021 update: just renovated. Feel much larger than before. Only 1 toilet inside the gym, and 2 changing room though. Looks more modern.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Because of the rude and uneducated staff (old man),he made the Active SG image very bad and ugly

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Excellent place to do your laps. They have a nice locker room, lockers for hire at 40 cents a go, benches to lay down on or place your stuff. The life guards are always watching out for you and I felt safe doing my weekly swim there. Do follow the ground rules though to go with the flow.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Did not personally visit the gym but wanted to give my sincerest thanks to their wonderful staff Adelin and Fariz (apologies if I misspelled your names), who helped my father during a bad fall outside the gym premise. Both staff members took prompt action upon seeing his fall and immediately brought a first aid kit and oxygen mask, reassuring my father while we were waiting for the ambulance. Thank you again 🙏

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Old Airport Road Food Centre exceeded my expectations with a great variety of Singapore heritage dishes. I also loved the creativity behind many stall names referencing places like Chong Pang, Kallang, and Blanco Court — adds local charm.

• Old Airport Road Fried Hokkien Mee (#01-99) – 4/5: Wet-style Hokkien Mee with decent wok hei, generous prawns, good value.

• Famous Old Airport Fried Oyster (#01-101) – 3/5: Soggy omelette, overcooked prawns, not worth $10.

• Chong Pang Huat BBQ (#01-90) – 3/5: Expensive, small portions, hygiene issues.

• Jin Fried Rice (#01-63) – 5/5: Fragrant mala fried rice with pork chop — excellent, must-try.

• Chuan Loo Bee Hokkien Food (#01-111) – 2/5: Tough pork ribs, odd sauce, disappointing.

• Whampoa Soya Bean (#01-68) – 4/5: Solid traditional desserts; soya drinks slightly diluted.

• Unkai Japanese Cuisine (#01-88) – 5/5: Generous salmon bowl, wallet-friendly, satisfying.

• Western Barbeque (#01-53) – 3/5: Chicken chop okay but lacks flavour; queues are long.

• Famous Kallang Airport Fried Oyster (#01-55) – 2/5: Oily, overwhelming, still hunting for a better version.

• Salt (#01-128) – 4/5: Aglio Olio with teriyaki chicken — decent but slightly pricey.

• Yi Ji Fried Prawn Noodles (#01-73) – 3.5/5: Carrot cake decent, noodles not outstanding.

• Xin Mei Xiang Lor Mee (#01-116) – 4/5: Double Fish Lor Mee decent; broth mediocre, some service issues.

• Heritage Tanglin Puff – 5/5: Singapore institution since 1952. Freshly fried puffs with generous fillings — curry potato and curry chicken are standouts. 6 for $12, great value.

• Ah Yee Hong Kong Roasted (#01-77) – 4/5: Three-meat combo rice ($10) — char siew well caramelised, roast pork had satisfying crackling, but roast duck was chewy and rice was dry. Chilli a nice touch. Would love a chicken option on the menu. Worth a visit for the char siew and roast pork.

Overall, a must-visit for variety and heritage flavours, though some stalls are hit-or-miss. Will keep exploring and updating!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Old Airport Road Food Center is one of popular places for breakfast and lunch.  There are many nice food around such as Lor Mee, Hokkien Mee, Prawn Noddle, Chicken Rice, Roasted Pork and Duck, Western Food, Wanton Noddle.  There is also a new NTUC at level 2.  This food Center can be quite packed during peaked hours.  If you are around Kallang area, I will recommend you guys to drop by …

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Great hawker centre but it can get a little stuffy. Try to grab one of the round tables by the entrances or near the ventilation areas for more space and cool air.

Loved the lor mee! Char koay teow and prawn mee we’re decent too.

If you’re getting ais kacang or cendol, get it from the popular stall in the middle to avoid disappointment!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Very nice and patient uncle who runs this stall. I always thought sugar canes all tasted the same, until I tried this. Happened to find a seat in front of this stall and decided to just buy from him. The sugar cane was really fresh and had a clean taste (there was not that sugary aftertaste). Uncle was also telling me that his is not soaked in sugar syrup and yet it was still naturally sweet! Highly recommended!

I think $2 for a cup, extra for lemon.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: One of my favourite food centres. Great selection and food. Easy to get to via Bus 36 from Changi. Due for a nice power cleaning though.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Hillion Mall is a lively suburban mall with a very wide variety of shops, dining options, and everyday services. One of its biggest strengths is the excellent connectivity - it is located right beside Bukit Panjang MRT, LRT, and the bus interchange, making it extremely convenient to visit even during rainy weather

The mall itself has quite a good mix of retail stores, cafes, bubble tea shops, supermarkets, and family-friendly spaces. I especially enjoyed exploring the Kopitiam Food Hall, which has a surprisingly wide range of food stalls and a lively atmosphere during meal times. There are also entertainment and arcade areas that make the mall feel more vibrant for families and students

The only downside is that the layout can feel a little confusing at times, especially for first-time visitors, as some sections and escalators are not very intuitive to navigate

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: My first time visiting this mall. Many shops and eateries in this mall. Will come again to try other food in this mall

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: From the looks of it, the area seems pretty accessible. There's a paved pathway leading to the seating area under the red archway, and it looks like there's enough space to maneuver a wheelchair around the tables. Are you planning to visit with someone who uses a wheelchair, or were you just curious about accessibility?

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This mall is super convenient because it’s an integrated hub with the MRT, LRT, and bus interchange all in one spot. The NTUC here is a major highlight because it’s huge and stays open late. The layout is a bit vertical, so you might spend a bit of time on escalators, but it’s a very functional place for a quick meal or grocery run.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Updating my review after visiting Hillion Mall again today. Unfortunately, the toilet condition has become even worse instead of improving.

One of the cubicles I entered had poop stains and splashes all over the toilet bowl and seat, making it extremely unpleasant and unhygienic to use. The basin area was also dirty and poorly maintained. Overall cleanliness was honestly quite disgusting.

I understand some users may behave irresponsibly, but proper cleaning and maintenance by the mall management is equally important. For a shopping mall, basic toilet hygiene standards should be maintained consistently. Hope management will seriously address this ongoing issue soon.

————————————————————————————-

Unacceptable hygiene standards at Hillion Mall. The toilets are consistently dirty, and overall cleanliness is clearly not being properly maintained. This appears to be an ongoing issue rather than a one-off incident. For a shopping mall, basic hygiene should be a minimum standard. Management needs to address this seriously and improve maintenance immediately.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Really like this library. There are many types of books and magazines to choose from, and it even has a second floor. The place is bright and clean. Staff are friendly and helpful. There is also a toilet and a baby changing diaper table, which is very convenient.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Clean, pleasant and quiet - truly a sanctuary for anyone who appreciates peaceful moments.

It's about a 15-minute walk from Ang Mo Kio MRT station, but I genuinely enjoy the stroll past the old shophouses along the way.
Very nostalgic.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Big, quiet, selection of books is awesome. Not as many desks as compared to other libraries. So if you're looking for a place to study, pls go elsewhere like punggol and don't hog the desks here.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: A nice public library. The library was quite secluded, and is about 1km walk from Ang Mo Kio MRT station. The library is 2 storeys high, and it's quite a quiet library with fewer patrons during off-peak hours. There were quite a number of empty tables at the Adult Fiction Section and power points were quite readily available. There was a water cooler at the 2nd storey. The place is highly recommended for students and readers who want a conducive environment. However it was slightly inaccessible and had some problems navigating from outside. But overall, I had a pleasant experience. Would come back again.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

The Ang Mo Kio library is very easy to find once you use Google maps. I think the library is an OK library, not horrible, not perfect. There are two levels. The first level has a children's section and adult fiction books. I'd say the adult fiction section is actually really nice. I read through a few of the books and I took multiple pictures to basically remind myself to check them out again.

The children's section isn't really a room; it's just some shelves out in the open. The second level has young adult books. The selection had some books that I've already read, and a lot that I have on my To-Be-Read (TBR).
There are some Chinese magazines, English fiction books, and Chinese books. More in the corner, there is a space where there are multiple black seats spaced apart. The second level has two tables with computers originally on it, and 6 seats for each of the tables.

There is a quiet reading lounge, and at the time I was there, there weren't that many people. But the layout is really nice because there are 9 seats where you can use your phone, read, and listen to an audiobook. But there’s also a few desks with seats where you can read, and maybe use your computer.

Overall, I think that the Ang Mo Kio Library is very well organized, spacious, has a nice quiet reading lounge, nice children’s section, and a good place to use your computer and read.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve is one of the best places in Singapore to experience nature in its most authentic form. From the moment I entered via the Neo Tiew Crescent entrance, I was surrounded by lush greenery, peaceful trails, and the soothing sounds of wildlife

The reserve is exceptionally well-maintained, with clear signage, wide boardwalks, and excellent visitor facilities at the Wetland Centre. The elevated wooden walkways make it easy to explore the mangrove forests while enjoying beautiful views of the surrounding wetlands

One of the highlights of my visit was observing mudskippers hopping across the mudflats among the mangrove roots. It was fascinating to see these unique creatures thriving in their natural habitat. The reserve offers plenty of opportunities to spot wildlife, so keep an eye out for monitor lizards, crabs, and migratory birds

I was also impressed by the Mudskipper Pod, a beautifully designed wooden observation structure that blends seamlessly into the environment. It provides a cool and shaded resting point with panoramic views over the mangroves and the Johor Strait. The architecture adds a unique touch to the overall experience without detracting from the natural surroundings

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A refreshing escape into nature! We enjoyed a slow and peaceful walk at Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve on May 27, fully immersed in the calm beauty of the wetlands. The walking paths were beautifully lined with lush trees, creating a naturally shaded and cooling environment that made the stroll especially comfortable even during the warmer hours.
The tranquil surroundings, gentle sounds of nature, and fresh air provided a relaxing break from the busy city atmosphere. Along the way, we appreciated the well-maintained trails and the opportunity to observe the rich biodiversity and greenery up close.
It is a wonderful place for a leisurely walk, nature appreciation, photography, or simply to unwind and recharge. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy a peaceful and scenic outdoor experience in Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A beautiful and peaceful escape into nature! The mangroves, fresh air, and rich wildlife make every walk feel special. Perfect for a relaxing stroll or bird watching—especially in the early morning or evening.
Stay curious and keep your eyes open… you might spot a giant monitor lizard crossing the path or even a crocodile resting in the water. A little adventure awaits you here!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: My family took a late afternoon walk down to Sungei Buloh during low tide today to unwind, and it was a great way to clear our heads. The air had that familiar, heavy scent of salt and damp mangrove mud, and with the water stripped back, the exposed api-api roots and glistening mudflats completely changed the landscape. We spent a while just watching the giant mudskippers drag themselves across the silt and bicker over territory, which is always entertaining. The low water also meant the local birds were out in full force. We managed to spot a Great Egret freezing in place before spearing a small fish, and a Grey Heron standing completely still in the shallows waiting for Archerfish. Finishing up on the main bridge during the golden hour was the highlight, looking down into the shallow river channel at schools of Halfbeaks skimming the surface under the evening sky was incredibly peaceful and a solid reminder of how nice it is to have this kind of wilderness right on our doorstep.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Really nice nature walk. There is one bus that goes there. Can spend about 3-4 hours there. You get to see the swamps, facing JB, lizards, fishes, crabs, birds etc.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
One of the old malls that had been revamped.

We were here years ago and after coming back bout 15years later, the place looks really different!

Lets dive in!

Location:
Integrated with the bus interchange and mrt, this mall is considered a hub for residents living in woodlands. There are plenty of entrances and is easily accessible everywhere.

Amenities:
Other than a furniture shop, you can find almost everything here. We were in awe and the food selections that we can find here. Not forgetting the gadget shops that have competitive prices. They also have a prayer room, which we were glad to use.

Parking:
Despite the many accesible options around, this mall has lots of parking lots available., subjected to non-peak hours yeah😅. Alternatively, there are also other nearby areas to park actually.

All in all, if you are up for an exercise to walk, this is one place to head down to. After walking on all floors, treat yourself to shopping and a good meal too!

Highly recommended from us!

Cheers!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Explored Causeway Point in a day. Anchor tenants like Metro Store, Courts for electrical and furniture, Uniqlo Japanese fashion wear. Lots of F&B selections from Kopitiam Bagus halal food court, Food Republic food court, Chinese, Japanese, Korean & western cuisines including lots of fried chicken - Texas, KFC, Jollibee, 4Fingers , Chagee, and lots of BbTea shops. Bakeries & Patisserie like Swee Heng, Annabelle, rrooll. You can spend a whole day exploring beautiful home decor at Madera and kitchen needs. To pamper yourselves with a pair of new glasses or shades from the few optical stores as well as beauty salons for hair and nails. Nostalgic snacks store, automated machines for the SGFR viral snack, Popmart machines can be found too.  From Causeway Point basement it will lead you to Woods Square and Woodlands Civic Center. Highly recommended to visit.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Wow many eateries at the basement offering good food here. Also first floor have a lot of nice shops selling food for takeaway all in one shopping mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Causeway Point is one of the older heartland malls in Singapore. Located just outside Woodlands MRT station, which is now an interchange to TEL line, and a bus interchange, it is in a very strategic location which explains why it’s never not busy. Causeway Point is also connected to Woodlands Civic Center and Woods Square. Beside it is also a location for occasional temporary Pasar Malam.

So, if you don’t like people, this place can be hellish. On the other side, it’s also a very convenient place to get everything you need from grocery, stationery, household items, electronic, pet stuff, food, everything you need.

The mall has everything, from Cinema to Yakun, Popular bookstore to FairPrice supermarket. There are many food varieties to choose from including two food courts. There are Metro department store and Courts. There are pharmacies like Watson, Guardian, and Unity.

Pros: You can find almost everything you need.

Cons: People! So many people!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Love ❤️ Causeway Point.

It’s near my house 🏠 and it has a wide variety of stores under one roof. Satisfy all my needs.

My daughter and I often went there to eat.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Here's a review you can use:

---

Ryan chou
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It is a great place to be with friends. We can enjoy the grennery and see the sea view of malaysua from here. It is quite hot in the afternoon so pls plan your trip here in the morning where it is not so hot. There is some bus stop near here but you can also walk here from woodlands mrt throguh Admiralty park. Not many facilities here except for the seafood restaurant and just greenery around.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Bugis Street is one of the best places to visit in Singapore for shopping, street food, and local vibes. The area is always lively and full of energy, with many affordable fashion items, souvenirs, snacks, and accessories to explore. Really suitable for tourists or anyone who enjoys walking around and discovering unique shops.

There are also many food stalls and nearby cafés around Bugis, making it a great place to relax after shopping. The atmosphere becomes even more interesting at night with the bright lights and busy crowd. Convenient location, easy to access by MRT, and a nice place to experience the modern and local side of Singapore at the same time. Highly recommended for shopping lovers and first-time visitors to Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I absolutely love this traditional market. It sells all kinds of goods, modern and traditional items, food and drinks, souvenirs, and various trinkets.

There's also a large fruit stall outside. If you're staying at a nearby hotel and craving fruit, this is definitely an excellent choice, incredible value and extremely fresh.

The food options here are overwhelming. The best way is to eat while you walk, and you can definitely eat until you're full. After you're full, there are still all kinds of desserts and drinks to enjoy.

This place can easily rival traditional markets and night markets from around the world. It's highly recommended for a visit!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The street is always bustling with energy, filled with countless small shops and stalls offering trendy clothes, accessories, souvenirs, and much more at affordable prices. It’s a great place for budget shopping and picking up unique items.
Even if you’re not planning to shop a lot, just walking through the market is an experience in itself. There are also plenty of food stalls and drink options around, so you can enjoy local snacks and refreshing juices while exploring.
It can get quite crowded, especially in the evenings, so be prepared for that. But overall, it’s a must-visit place in Singapore for shopping lovers and anyone who enjoys vibrant street markets!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: We went to check on the souvenir prices and among all souvenir places we’ve been, we think bugis street has the best prices already. Super affordable and not so much crowd. You can haggle a little and have some food and snacks that you can try around while shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Bugis Street is a lively and bustling shopping spot that a lot of visitors recommend for a fun and budget-friendly experience. Many people love how wide and varied the shops are, with everything from affordable fashion and accessories to quirky souvenirs, making it a great place to explore and shop without spending too much.
The street food stalls and snack options add to the vibrant atmosphere — you can easily spend some time tasting local bites while browsing through the stalls. It does get crowded, especially in the evenings and on weekends, but that’s part of the energetic vibe here.
Overall, it’s a great place for casual shopping, good street food, and vibrant street life — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Mustafa Centre is an incredible shopping destination! I always find exactly what I need there, and the variety of items available is truly amazing. 😊 The atmosphere is lively, and it's a great place to explore. So many unique finds! 🛍️ Love it! ✨

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A 24/7 Shopping Paradise with Great Prices! 🛒🇸🇬
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Mustafa Centre is one of the most famous shopping malls in Singapore, and for a good reason. It’s open 24/7, making it incredibly convenient for anyone to shop at any time of the day or night.
Why I recommend it:
• Best Prices: Almost all goods are sold at very reasonable and competitive prices.
• One-Stop Shop: From electronics and jewelry to groceries and souvenirs, you can find everything here. It’s the go-to spot for foreigners and migrant workers to buy gifts and essentials for their families back home before vacation.
Important Tip for Shoppers:
I highly recommend checking the expiry dates on all food and consumable items before you buy. Try to ensure they have at least one year of validity.
My Experience: I’ve had issues multiple times where I bought items in bulk, but since the expiry date was too close, I couldn't finish them in time. This is a big waste of money, so please double-check your basket before heading to the cashier!
#MustafaCentre #SingaporeShopping
#LittleIndia
#TravelTips
#BudgetShopping #SmartShopping

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I have been to Singapore several times, but it was my first time going there.
I really regret that I didn't go there before.
There are many snacks, accessories, cosmetics and so on.
As a tourist, I could have reasonable souvenirs.
I will go there next time when I visit Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Mustafa Centre is one of the most unique shopping experiences in Singapore. Located in the heart of Little India, this massive multi-level store literally has everything — from electronics and perfumes to groceries, souvenirs, jewelry, clothing, and even travel essentials.
What makes it even more impressive is that it’s open 24 hours a day. Whether you need something late at night or just enjoy browsing without crowds during off-peak hours, it’s incredibly convenient.
The store can feel a bit overwhelming at first because it’s packed floor-to-ceiling with products and narrow aisles, but that’s part of its charm. Prices are competitive, and you can often find great deals, especially on chocolates, cosmetics, and electronics.
If you’re staying in the Little India area or nearby, it’s definitely worth a visit — even if just to see how massive and diverse the selection is. A true Singapore experience for shoppers.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Mammoth! You name it and you'll have it. It spans across the street and variety. Reasonable prices and lot of variety. They have every retail brand under the sky here. Electronics, apparels, grocery, fresh produce, jewellery what not. Hats off to the management. If you are looking for best prices in Singapore this is the place to go.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Nice, quiet and with lots of books. Highly recommended library of you love reading!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Amazing! Just no words to say. It was my first time to the Jurong regional library and the very scale of it took me in awe. So many collections of different books and so many different levels to go and explore! They have a community space for events and workshops , then there is a cafe and bakery inside with good food options. There is so many different things to explore! I couldn’t even finish going through all the sections! I will definitely go there again soon. The place is also located very close to the MRT  station and the Jurong east bus terminus . It has a sheltered walkway so we could easily reach the place even though it was raining. Many people from all different age groups were there. There is study tables with charging points. For some of the study tables you need to book in advance but for the rest you can always find and empty space. There are DVDs and other stuff as well. If you have to borrow books you need to register , but I just went for reading so I didn’t have to register anything. The library closes at 9pm and is open all days of the week. They will announce over the mike before the closing time so you have time to pack your stuff. I especially liked the photography section and farming section . There’s a separate magazine section and newspaper section as well. Different languages from Tamil to Malay to Korean you will find in the library .

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: This library was visited few times in the span of 15 years. Still impressed by its cleanliness, calm and  peaceful atmosphere in addition to the silent air, the display of new arrivals also catch my eyes. Always full of study-loaded students and other book-lovers, you will still find a seat for enjoying some books or surfing internet on the provided PC's as the library is very spacious. Remarkably, there is a vast collection of books.

review_text: Spacious JE Centre Library with many seats availability. Cool Air-Cond Environment, Front Welcoming Display, Information,  Photo Wall Taking, Green Wall Digital Huge Screen & Clean well maintained Toilet. Great Place for Reading, Desktop PC Computer Internet access & comfy sofas. Wonderful!👏🇸🇬

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.
